structured_payload_candidate.title: Fault Response for Fans, Pumps, and Cooling Towers
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Upon fault: command the next off equipment (B) ON while alarming equipment remains ON. If B also alarms, repeat until the required quantity is met or no more available. Move alarming equipment to last in staging order (reverse chronologically). Non-alarming equipment follow even wear order. Alarming equipment will run if called for by stage.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: i. For fans, pumps, and cooling towers: (a) The next commanded off equipment in the staging order, Equipment “B”, shall be commanded on while alarming Equipment “A” remains commanded on. (b) If Equipment “B” fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), it shall remain commanded on and the preceding step shall be repeated until the quantity of equipment called for by the current stage has proven on, or there are no more available equipment. (c) Set alarming equipment to the last positions in the lead/lag or lead/standby staging order sequenced reverse chronologically (i.e., the equipment that alarmed most recently is sent to last position). (d) Staging order of non-alarming equipment shall follow the even wear logic. Equipment in alarm can only automatically move up on the staging order if another equipment goes into alarm. (e) Equipment in alarm shall run if so called for by the lead/lag or lead/standby staging order and present stage.

evidence_quote: i. structured_payload_candidate.title: Fault Response for Chillers and Boilers
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Upon fault: command alarming equipment OFF and immediately set it to last in staging order; command the next off equipment ON. If it also faults, repeat until enough non-alarming equipment proven on or all tried. If insufficient, most recently alarming equipment remains on. Non-alarming equipment follow even wear order.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: ii. For chillers and boilers: (a) The next commanded off equipment in the staging order, Equipment “B”, shall be commanded on while alarming Equipment “A” is commanded off and set to the last position in the lead/lag staging order. (b) If Equipment B fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), repeat the preceding step until the quantity of equipment called for by the lead/lag logic have proven on or until all equipment has been tried. (c) If all equipment has been tried and the quantity of non-alarming equipment is less than called for then the most recently alarmed equipment will remain commanded on. (d) Staging order of non-alarming equipment shall follow the even wear logic. Equipment in alarm can only automatically move up in the staging order if another equipment goes into alarm. (e) Equipment in alarm shall run if so called for by the lead/lag staging order and present stage.

evidence_quote: ii. For chillers and boilers: (a) The next commanded off equipment in the staging order, Equipment “B”, shall be commanded on while alarming Equipment “A” is commanded off and set to the last position in the lead/lag staging order. (b) If Equipment B fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), repeat the preceding step until the quantity of equipment called for by the lead/lag logic have proven on or until all equipment has been tried. (c) If all equipment has been tried and the quantity of non-alarming equipment is less than called for then the most recently alarmed equipment will remain commanded on. (d) Staging order of non-alarming equipment shall follow the even wear logic. Equipment in alarm can only automatically move up in the staging order if another equipment goes into alarm. (e) Equipment in alarm shall run if so called for by the lead/lag staging order and present stage.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan/Pump Hand Operation Detection
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If the status point does not match its ON/OFF point for 15 seconds after a time delay of 60 seconds when the equipment is commanded OFF, treat as ON-in-hand, set as lead, and generate a Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: status mismatch 15 sec after 60 sec delay when commanded OFF
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: set equipment to lead, generate Level 4 alarm
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: i. Status point not matching its ON/OFF point for 15 seconds after a time delay of 60 seconds when the equipment is commanded OFF.

evidence_quote: i. Status point not matching its ON/OFF point for 15 seconds after a time delay of 60 seconds when the equipment is commanded OFF.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Maintenance Mode Alarm Suppression
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When a device is placed in maintenance mode, all associated alarms are suppressed except life-safety alarms; a daily Level 3 alarm is generated to remind that the device is in maintenance mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: suppress alarms (except life-safety), generate daily Level 3 reminder
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Maintenance Mode. Operators shall have the ability to put any device (e.g., AHU) in/out of maintenance mode. a. All alarms associated with a device in maintenance mode will be suppressed. Exception: Life safety alarms shall not be suppressed. b. If a device is in maintenance mode, issue a daily Level 3 alarm at a scheduled time indicating that the device is still in maintenance mode.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Trim & Respond Setpoint Reset Logic
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: upstream system/plant setpoint controlled by T&R loop
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the associated device is proven ON (after delay Td), every time step T: if R ≤ I, trim setpoint by SPtrim; if R > I, change setpoint by (R – I)*SPres, capped at SPres-max. Setpoint is bounded between SPmin and SPmax; when device is OFF, setpoint = SP0.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: cycle T&R adjustment every T, respond to requests as described
configurable_values: SP0, SPmin, SPmax, Td, T, I, SPtrim, SPres, SPres-max (as defined in Table 5.1.14.3)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Trim & Respond logic shall reset the setpoint within the range SPmin to SPmax. When the associated device is OFF, the setpoint shall be SP0. The reset logic shall be active while the associated device is proven ON, starting Td after initial device start command. When active, every time step T, if R≤I, trim the setpoint by SPtrim. If there are more than I requests, respond by changing the setpoint by SPres *(R – I), (i.e., the number of requests minus the number of ignored requests) but no more than SPres-max.

evidence_quote: See Section 5.1.14.4  for an example of T&R implementation.

evidence_text: See Section 5.1.14.4  for an example of T&R implementation.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Trim & Respond Request Multiplication and Sending
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Multiply the number of requests determined from zone/system logic by the Importance-Multiplier and send the product to the upstream system/plant that serves the zone/system.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: multiply requests by Importance-Multiplier, send upstream
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Multiply the number of requests determined from zone/system logic times the Importance-Multiplier and send to the system/plant that serves the zone/system.

structured_payload_candidate.title: ASHRAE 90.1 Fixed Dry Bulb Economizer High Limit (Majority Climates)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When using fixed dry bulb high limit in climate zones 1b,2b,3b,3c,4b,4c,5b,5c,6b,7,8, disable economizer when outdoor air temperature exceeds 24°C (75°F).
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: outdoor air temperature > 24°C (75°F) and applicable climate zone

structured_payload_candidate.title: Time-Based Alarm Suppression Default Delay
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Calculate a time-delay period after any setpoint change based on the difference between the controlled variable and the new setpoint. Default: 18 minutes per °C (10 min per °F), up to 120 minutes max.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: suppress alarms/reset requests for calculated delay
configurable_values: default delay 18 min/°C (10 min/°F), max 120 min
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Calculate a time-delay period after any change in setpoint based on the difference between the controlled variable (e.g., zone temperature) at the time of the change and the new setpoint. The default time delay period shall be as follows: a. For thermal zone temperature alarms: 18 minutes per °C (10 minutes per °F) of difference but no longer than 120 minutes
